Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions And Costs In The Amount Of $3,577.65 Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Of Record
Off Calendar Oct-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SANG H. PARK, Esq. of Marlis Park, P.C. (Tel: 323-922-2000) for plaintiffs;
Atty. JOONG Y. IM, Esq. of Law Office of Joong Y. Im (Tel: 415-775-9854) for defendant John Yoon dba Kazan.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JIEUN OH's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions And Costs In The Amount Of $3,577.65 Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Of Record

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted.

Plaintiffs Youngmi Lee and Jieun Oh have filed separate motions to compel further responses which differ only as to the dates of alleged deficiency in the defendant's responses. This tentative ruling applies to both motions.
Defendant Yoon's responses fail to "specify the writings from which the answer [to interrogatory] may be derived or ascertained" as required under Code of Civil Procedure Section 2030.220 but refer only general and non-specific categories, "the wage records" and (unsworn) "the canceled checks". This failure is exacerbated by the defendant's failure to produce responsive records for the entirety of the employment period of either plaintiff. The defendant's assertion that plaintiffs were paid at least minimum wages for the periods of missing records addresses the rate but not the amount of wages as sought in the interrogatories and is non-responsive to the interrogatory.

Accordingly, Defendant Yoon shall provide further code-compliant responses without objection within 20 days of the date of order. Sanctions are denied in view of Defendant Yoon's apparent good faith but insufficient attempt to provide the discovery sought.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
